posts.text: Why are central banks buying so much gold? A record 90% of central banks cited gold's performance during times of crisis as a key factor in their decision to hold gold, according to the World Gold Council survey of 69 central banks. This trend is more frequently seen in emerging market and developing economy central banks, with 92% citing crisis performance as the primary driver. By comparison, this figure stands at 81% among advanced economy central banks. Furthermore, gold's role as a long-term store of value and inflation hedge was cited by 84% of respondents, while 83% pointed to its effectiveness as a portfolio diversifier. Meanwhile, 85% of emerging market central banks cited gold as a geopolitical hedge, compared to just 56% in advanced economies. Gold remains at the core of central bank financial reserves.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter
